items.question: This bridge ___ in 1932.
explanations: 'Built' alone has no 'be' — the passive needs it.
Correct! Past passive = was/were + past participle: 'was built'.
'Was build' uses the base verb; use the participle 'built'.
'Is building' is active continuous, not passive.

items.question: Make it passive: 'They make these shoes in Italy.'
options: These shoes are made in Italy.
These shoes are make in Italy.
These shoes is made in Italy.
explanations: Correct! Present passive = are + past participle: 'are made'.
'Make' has no 'be' and keeps the active verb.
'Are make' uses the base verb; use 'made'.
'Shoes' is plural, so it needs 'are', not 'is'.

items.question: My car ___ last week — I've reported it to the police.
explanations: 'Stole' is active past; here the car receives the action.
Correct! Past passive: 'My car was stolen'.
'Was stole' uses the base/past form; the participle is 'stolen'.
'Has stolen' is active present perfect.

items.question: The report ___ already ___ to all staff.
explanations: 'Has sent' is active and misses 'been'.
Correct! Present perfect passive = has been + participle: 'has been sent'.
'The report' is singular, so use 'has', not 'have'.
'Was send' uses the base verb; the participle is 'sent'.

items.question: Which sentence correctly adds the doer?
options: The Mona Lisa was painted by Leonardo.
The Mona Lisa was painted from Leonardo.
The Mona Lisa painted by Leonardo.
The Mona Lisa was paint by Leonardo.
explanations: Correct! The agent takes 'by': 'was painted by Leonardo'.
The agent takes 'by', not 'from'.
This is missing 'was' — no passive without 'be'.
'Was paint' uses the base verb; use 'painted'.

items.question: Choose the sentence that should stay ACTIVE (can't be passive).
explanations: Correct! 'Happen' has no object, so it can't be passive.
'Clean' has an object (the office) → 'The office was cleaned'.
'Open' has an object (the door) → 'The door was opened'.
'Hire' has an object (her) → 'She was hired'.

items.question: Coffee ___ in over 70 countries.
explanations: 'Grows' would mean coffee grows itself; here people grow it.
Correct! Present passive: 'Coffee is grown in over 70 countries'.
'Is grow' uses the base verb; use 'grown'.
'Grown' alone has no 'be'.

items.question: Correct this: 'The window broke by the boys.' (they did it)
options: The window was broken by the boys.
The window was break by the boys.
The window broke by the boys.
The window is broke by the boys.
explanations: Correct! With a doer, use the passive: 'was broken by the boys'.
'Was break' uses the base verb; use 'broken'.
'Broke by' has no 'be' — not a correct passive.
'Is broke' is not standard; use 'was broken'.

items.question: A decision ___ at the meeting tomorrow.
explanations: 'Will make' is active and needs a subject that decides.
Correct! Future passive = will be + participle: 'will be made'.
'Will be make' uses the base verb; use 'made'.
'Is made' is present, but 'tomorrow' needs the future.

items.question: When is the passive the BEST choice?
options: When the doer is unknown or unimportant.
When you want to name who did it.
When the verb has no object.
When the sentence is a command.
explanations: Correct! Use the passive when the doer is unknown, obvious or unimportant.
If naming the doer matters, the active is often clearer.
Verbs with no object can't be passive at all.
Commands are normally active (Close the door).
